question: Explain the fundamental difference between a one-way ANOVA and a two-way ANOVA. What additional hypothesis can a two-way ANOVA test that a one-way ANOVA cannot?
expectedPoints: One-way ANOVA analyzes the effect of a single factor on a response variable.
Two-way ANOVA analyzes the simultaneous effect of two factors on a response variable.
A two-way ANOVA can test for an interaction effect between the two factors, determining if the effect of one factor depends on the level of the other factor.

question: Describe the purpose of the Design of Experiments (DOE) in data science. List two key principles of a well-designed experiment.
expectedPoints: The purpose of DOE is to plan and conduct experiments efficiently to obtain valid and objective conclusions about the factors affecting a system or process.
Key principles include: Randomization (randomly assigning treatments to avoid bias) and Replication (repeating experiments to estimate variability and improve precision).

question: A computer scientist is testing three new sorting algorithms (A, B, C) against a standard one (S). They run each algorithm on multiple different datasets and record the execution time. After performing a one-way ANOVA, they reject the null hypothesis. Describe the necessary steps they must take next to determine which specific algorithms differ from each other and from the standard. Justify why simply performing multiple t-tests is an invalid approach.
expectedPoints: After a significant ANOVA, post-hoc tests (e.g., Tukey's HSD, Bonferroni) must be conducted to make pairwise comparisons between all means (A vs S, B vs S, C vs S, A vs B, etc.).
Performing multiple t-tests without adjustment increases the family-wise error rate, greatly inflating the chance of a Type I error (falsely concluding a difference exists).
Post-hoc procedures control the overall significance level for the entire set of comparisons, maintaining the experiment-wide error rate.
